text: Plant Demeter's sacred seed in the courtyard
outcome: You dig the soil with your own hands. The seed slips in — small, dark, humming with something ancient. Demeter smiles. 'Now we wait. Growth cannot be rushed. But when it comes, your land will remember this moment.'

text: Ask Demeter to restore the dying orchard
outcome: She touches the oldest tree — a gnarled thing that has not borne fruit in a decade. It shudders. A single apple falls into her hand. 'This orchard will live. But you will owe the earth a harvest. Not for me — for the land itself.'

text: Learn the cycles of the land from Demeter
outcome: She kneels in the dirt beside you — a goddess, kneeling. 'The earth does not care that you are a bastard. It only cares that you show up. That you learn its rhythms. That you tend what is given to you.' You learn. The fields will show the difference.

text: Negotiate a tribute of grain to secure Demeter's blessing
outcome: You offer a portion of every harvest — not to Demeter, but to the village, in her name. She is surprised. 'You bargain for your people, not yourself. That is... unusual in a lord. I accept. Your fields will prosper. Your people will eat.'
